content.full_text:
JAMES S. MARTIN
PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION
100 PARK PLACE, SUITE 168
SAN RAMON, CALIFORNIA 94583
(415) 831-0168


September 5, 1991


Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

As you will see from the enclosed photocopy of the ballot, an
election to an Alameda County special district will take place on
November 5, 1991. The purpose of this letter is to determine the
propriety of a mailing which the district intends during October,
1991, to explain some recent events which bear upon the charges
being imposed upon the rate-payers in connection with the
district's compliance with waste stream diversion mandates imposed
by AB 939.

At its September 3, 1991 regular board meeting, the district
directors unanimously determined that an informational letter
should be sent to the district rate payors because of a series of
events largely the result of an Alameda County Recycling Initiative
which imposed what appears to be a duplication of fees for a
curbside recycling program initiated by the district in April,
1991. As you will note from the enclosed draft of the mailer, a
Superior Court proposed ruling as to the invalidity of the
Recycling Initiative has resulted in considerable uncertainty as to
the ultimate costs of the waste stream diversion. This is
unprecedented in my 17 years as a member of the board.

It is intended that the mailer be sent to the rate-payors in
October, when the Judge's proposed ruling becomes a judgment.
Because that will be within two or three weeks of the November 5th
election, the question has arisen as to whether this communication
with the rate-payors-voters violates any of the fair political
practice standards of the State of California.

The letter is to be signed by the District Manager and paid
for by budgeted district funds.


[The middle section contains several paragraphs of text, but the individual words and sentences are largely illegible due to the poor image quality]

[There appear to be multiple paragraphs separated by spacing, suggesting this is a formal document or letter]

[At the bottom of the visible text, there may be a signature or closing statement, but it is not legible]

[Overall, while the document appears to be a formal typed document with multiple paragraphs, the image quality makes it impossible to accurately transcribe the specific text content.]

Mr. Scott Hallabrin
September 5, 1991
Page Two


     The Board of Directors would like to take action on this
matter at its next meeting, September 19, 1991. Accordingly, we
would appreciate your immediate consideration and response.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        JAMES S. MARTIN

JSM:rm

Enclosures

CASTRO VALLEY SANITARY DISTRICT

DONALD H. STROOT, PRESIDENT                 21040 MARSHALL STREET • CASTRO VALLEY, CALIFORNIA 94546-6098 • TELEPHONE (415) 537-0757
JAMES S. MARTIN, SECRETARY
ARTHUR L. VARGAS
ALFRED M. AITON
ANTHONY MORSILLI

MARY E. FREDETTE, DIST. MGR.

DRAFT


WHAT'S GOING ON WITH OUR RECYCLING PROGRAM?

     Well you might ask.  Remember back in early 1990 when the
Legislature told us we would have to reduce the waste stream into
our dump sites by 25% by 1995, and 50% by 2000?  Since almost
everyone thought that was a good idea, your District awarded a 5-
year contract to a Browning Ferris curbside recycler which was the
low bidder at $1.49 per month per household.  About the same time
"Measure D" (the Recycling Initiative) was put on the ballot by
persons who wanted to raise money for parallel waste stream
diversion projects.  Although the Initiative was opposed by your
Board of Directors, it passed in a county-wide election in
November, 1990 and resulted in an addition to your monthly garbage
bill of about $1.25.

     On August 17, 1991, your Board, having learned that we were to
receive about $80,000 in Measure D monies, resolved to reduce the
$1.49 to $1.00 per month.  On the same day, unknown to your Board,
a Superior Court Judge ruled that the Recycling Initiative was
invalid on several grounds and that the collection of the $6.00 per
ton surcharge was improper.

     Because the proponents of the Recycling Initiative are talking
appeal of the judgment, your Board has directed a Resolution to the
litigants urging them to accept the judicial determination.  What
we are hoping for is that the dump site surcharge will be dropped
and the monies collected (over $3,000,000) returned to all of the
rate-payors in the county who have experienced this duplication of
charges.

     Finally, thanks for your patience in those areas where the
Pleasant Hill Recycling Company has failed to coordinate the pick-
up days with the garbage trucks.  They promised that they have all
the necessary information now so you can [illegible] expect to have the
recycling bins picked up the same day as your garbage cans are
emptied.
